preview_text:
The model favors South Korea at 62%, leaving South Africa with only a 13% chance of victory. The draw carries a modest 25% probability, suggesting competitive but not evenly matched play.

The win-loss spread shows South Korea as a clear but not overwhelming favorite. A draw remains plausible at roughly one-in-four, indicating the match could unfold with either side capable of forcing a stalemate, though a South Korea victory is the most probable single outcome. South Africa's path to three points is narrow but not negligible.

The model and market are closely aligned. Sportsbooks assign South Korea a 60% win probability against the model's 62%, a gap of 2 percentage points. Polymarket pricing shows similar consensus, with spreads under 3 percentage points across all three outcomes. No meaningful divergence exists between the statistical forecast and market pricing on this fixture.
